ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific salsiccia-gorgonzola pizza subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific salsiccia-gorgonzola variant exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for salsiccia gorgonzola pizza.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole individual pizza of approximately 300 g, typical of a restaurant-style Italian pizza served at Mingle - Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, NS as to type of crust (SR Legacy 21299) and component ingredient data for gorgonzola cheese and Italian sausage
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for salsiccia-gorgonzola pizza. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for meat pizza (21299) as a base, adjusted upward for fat and protein to reflect the dual-cheese load (gorgonzola DOP + grana padano) and fennel pork sausage topping. Gorgonzola cheese nutrient profile sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 173420. Italian pork sausage from USDA FDC ID 167904. Weighted blend applied proportionally to a typical pizza composition (dough ~40%, sauce/tomato ~10%, cheese ~30%, sausage ~20% by weight).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for salsiccia-gorgonzola pizza. GI of 50 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza with cheese and meat toppings (GI ~50, referenced in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables). Thin-crust meat-and-cheese pizza consistently shows GI in the range 45–55 due to fat and protein blunting glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 75) / 100 = 37.5, based on 300 g serving with 25 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~1860 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium load from gorgonzola DOP, grana padano, and cured pork sausage; a single serving may exceed 80% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~50) but high glycemic load per serving (~37.5 GL units) due to large portion size
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions; while the GI is moderate-low, the high carbohydrate load per full serving will produce a significant postprandial glucose rise, and the high saturated fat content may worsen insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.2 g per 100 g) and high sodium from multiple full-fat cheeses and pork sausage
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this pizza as an occasional food; the combination of saturated fat from gorgonzola, grana padano, and fior di latte, together with high sodium from cured sausage, presents a compounded cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salsiccia Gorgonzola Pizza is an Italian-style pizza featuring a wheat-flour base topped with fior di latte mozzarella, fennel-seasoned pork sausage (salsiccia), gorgonzola DOP (a protected-designation Italian blue cheese), cherry tomatoes, and grana padano (a hard aged Italian cheese). The pizza is baked at high temperature, producing a crisp or slightly charred crust with a soft, cheese-laden interior. The flavour profile is bold and savoury: the pungent, creamy gorgonzola contrasts with the fennel-spiced sausage, while the grana padano adds a salty, umami depth and the cherry tomatoes provide acidity. In Singapore, this dish is served at Italian restaurants such as Mingle - Funan and is priced at the premium end of the casual-dining pizza segment (~SGD 34). Nutritionally, the pizza is energy-dense (~272 kcal per 100 g), rich in protein (~12.8 g) and fat (~13.5 g, of which ~6.2 g is saturated), and provides moderate carbohydrate (~25 g). The dual-cheese and cured-meat topping results in a very high sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low-moderate), but the glycemic load per full serving is high (~37.5), which is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or halal diets, and contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a salsiccia gorgonzola pizza?
What is fior di latte on pizza?
Sodium content of Italian sausage pizza

confidence_reason: No exact match for salsiccia-gorgonzola pizza exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB, USDA SR Legacy, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted blending of USDA FoodData Central component data for meat pizza, gorgonzola cheese, and Italian pork sausage. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA component sources used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component sources used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component blend used for this estimation. All other -1 fields reflect absence of data in the approved source blend rather than confirmed zero values. Food group retained as 'Grains and staples' consistent with input classification, as pizza is a grain-based dish. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' is appropriate for pizza. This record was generated from a field survey data seed record for Mingle - Funan restaurant. The dish is not a Singaporean dish by origin; it is Italian, so multilingual requirements apply only to Italian cuisine (no mandatory Chinese/Malay/Tamil/Hindi names required). GI category 'low' is correct per the validator threshold (GI 50 ≤ 55 = low).
